entries.summary: First evidence-backed market refresh and dashboard scoring pass.
details: Promoted Audi A6 Sportback e-tron to shortlist because it is strong on range, charging, integrated dashboard, B&O audio option and premium fit.
Kept Smart #5 Brabus on shortlist because it strongly matches sound and performance, but flagged screen/control, phone integration, support and security as validation risks.
Moved Volvo EX30 from shortlist to watchlist because the central-screen/minimal-controls philosophy conflicts with dashboard and physical-control preferences despite excellent performance/value.
Added Volvo ES90 as a high-potential but expensive watchlist car: strong sound, power, range and charging; dashboard and cost need validation.
Kept MG IM5 and IM6 in research despite exceptional specs because quality, controls, phone integration, security and UK support remain insufficiently proven.
Added visible key facts and source summaries to the dashboard cards.

entries.summary: Moved from flat CSV-only tracking toward structured data plus static dashboard.
details: Added sound/audio as the highest-weighted criterion.
Clarified that high performance is desirable, not a penalty, provided the car has usable comfort modes.
Added dashboard/control ergonomics as an explicit criterion, including dislike of non-integrated tablet-style screens.
Added reliable phone integration as a critical criterion.
Added security/theft resistance as a first-class criterion.
Kept Tesla excluded while allowing Chinese-brand cars if quality evidence is strong.
